title: Understanding the Pythagorean Theorem
text: The Pythagorean Theorem is a fundamental principle in geometry that relates the lengths of the sides of a right triangle. It states that in a right triangle, the square of the length of the hypotenuse (the side opposite the right angle) is equal to the sum of the squares of the lengths of the other two sides.

title: Identifying the Right Triangle
text: To apply the Pythagorean Theorem, you need to identify which side is the hypotenuse. In a right triangle, the hypotenuse is always the longest side.

title: Applying the Pythagorean Theorem
text: If you have a right triangle with sides of lengths a, $$b$$, and c, where c is the hypotenuse, the Pythagorean Theorem can be written as: $$a^2+b^2=c^2$$. Use this formula to find the missing side.

title: Checking Your Work
text: After calculating the length of the missing side, check your work by substituting the values back into the Pythagorean Theorem to ensure the equation holds true.
